description: Fixed prompt from passive/exploratory (V1) to request-type language. V2 prompt uses "person who needs practical help and guidance" so benign conversations also contain request markers like could you, how do I.

analysis.v1_vs_v2_comparison: V1 separation was driven by request-marker presence/absence (benign had no request language). V2 fixed this: benign now has request markers (could you, how to, want to). However, V2 separation is now driven by formality gap: benign uses formal/structured request language while jailbreak uses casual/informal style (just, hey, really, like, what if).
analysis.implication: The prompt fix successfully eliminated the request-marker asymmetry but the user simulator generates overly formal benign conversations compared to the casual style of jailbreak user simulators. A V3 would need to match register/formality.

sample_conversations.topic: file encryption best practices and data security
sample_conversations.first_user_msg: Could you help me with setting up file encryption for my personal computer? I want to make sure my sensitive documents are protected...

sample_conversations.topic: pharmaceutical chemistry and over-the-counter drug regulation
sample_conversations.first_user_msg: Could you help me understand how to properly identify and classify over-the-counter (OTC) drugs based on their active ingredients?...
